name: Double pulsar PSR J0737-3039A/B precision tests
requirement: Any candidate framework must pass the multi-parameter consistency tests provided by the unique double-pulsar system PSR J0737-3039A/B, in which both neutron stars are observed as pulsars. Seven post-Keplerian parameters and the mass ratio give six independent tests of GR; all consistent at the 0.05% level on the most stringent, P_b_dot.

regime.description: Double-neutron-star binary, orbital period 2.45 hours, eccentricity 0.088, inclination ~89 degrees (nearly edge-on).

current_bound.value: P_b_dot_obs / P_b_dot_GR = 0.99963 +/- 0.00063; Shapiro delay shape s = 0.99977 +/- 0.00010
current_bound.experiment: Long-term timing at Parkes, Green Bank, Westerbork, MeerKAT
current_bound.reference: Kramer et al., 'Strong-field Gravity Tests with the Double Pulsar', PRX 11, 041050 (2021), arXiv:2112.06795

what_it_excludes: Provides the most precise strong-field test of GR currently available, including bounds on dipolar GW emission, gravitomagnetism, and Lense-Thirring precession of the orbital plane. Constrains scalar-tensor theories and effective-field-theory deviations from GR with unprecedented precision in the strong-field regime.

parameterization.name: Parameterized Post-Keplerian, with seven measured parameters: omega_dot, gamma_PPK, r, s (Shapiro), P_b_dot, and the Damour-Schaefer mass ratio R = m_A/m_B from observing both pulsars.
parameterization.reference: Damour and Deruelle, Ann. Inst. H. Poincare A 44, 263 (1986); Damour and Taylor, PRD 45, 1840 (1992); Kramer et al., PRX 11, 041050 (2021).
parameterization.summary: Both component masses are over-determined by the seven PPK observables, providing six independent consistency tests of GR.

procedural.predicted_observable: Each PPK parameter has a closed-form GR prediction in (m_A, m_B, K, e). The framework must reproduce all seven simultaneously.
procedural.bound_in_parameterization: Tightest single test: P_b_dot agreement at 0.063%. Combined chi^2 across all PPK parameters provides the cleanest existing bound on strong-field deviations from GR.
framework_inputs: All inputs from binary_pulsar_psr_b1913_16, plus the framework's prediction for the Shapiro time delay shape s (sensitive to gamma) and for the relativistic spin-orbit precession of pulsar B.

primary_references.citation: Kramer et al., 'Strong-field Gravity Tests with the Double Pulsar', PRX 11, 041050 (2021)
Burgay et al., 'An increased estimate of the merger rate of double neutron stars from observations of a highly relativistic system', Nature 426, 531 (2003)
